text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English. I'm Phil. And I'm Georgie.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。我是Phil。我是Georgie。

text: Now, you probably know the word wellbeing – it's a general feeling of health and happiness. But have you ever heard of flourishing?
zh: 你可能知道wellbeing(幸福感)这个词——它指一种总体上健康快乐的感觉。但你听说过flourishing(蓬勃发展)这个词吗?

text: It's an idea which Claudia Hammond has been investigating for BBC Radio 4 programme all in the mind.
zh: 这是Claudia Hammond为BBC第四台节目《All in the Mind》一直在探讨的一个概念。

text: I wonder if you all feel that you are flourishing in life.
zh: 我很想知道,你们是否觉得自己的生活正在蓬勃发展。

text: This is another psychological buzzword with added evidence.
zh: 这是另一个有实证支持的心理学流行词。

text: Now, if you've not heard that term before, it's not just about well -being, it's about your whole life being good.
zh: 如果你以前没听过这个词,它指的不仅仅是幸福感,而是你整个生活状态都很好。

text: Flourishing is a psychological buzzword,
zh: Flourishing是一个心理学流行词,

text: a word or phrase that has become fashionable and is being used a lot, especially on television and in newspapers.
zh: 指一个变得时髦、被广泛使用的词或短语,尤其常见于电视和报纸中。

text: People who are flourishing feel feel good about all aspects of their life,
zh: 蓬勃发展的人对生活的方方面面都感觉良好,

text: including happiness, health, relationships and financial security.
zh: 包括幸福感、健康、人际关系和经济保障。

text: Do you feel you're flourishing, Georgie? Yeah, I think I'm quite lucky actually.
zh: Georgie,你觉得自己的生活在蓬勃发展吗?是的,我觉得自己其实挺幸运的。

text: I really like my job. I have a good family and friends.
zh: 我很喜欢我的工作,也有很好的家人和朋友。

text: Yeah, everything's going well. What about you, Phil? I think the same.
zh: 嗯,一切都进展顺利。那你呢,Phil?我也差不多。

text: I think I'm happy and I've got everything I need. Lovely.
zh: 我觉得自己很快乐,该有的都有了。真好。

text: In this episode, we'll hear about the Global Flourishing Study,
zh: 在这一集里,我们将了解“全球蓬勃发展研究”,

text: a new survey into the lives of over 200 ,000 people in 22 countries.
zh: 这是一项针对22个国家、超过20万人生活状况的新调查。

text: We'll learn some useful new words and phrases. And remember,
zh: 我们还会学到一些实用的新词和短语。别忘了,

text: you'll find all this episode's vocabulary on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 本集所有词汇都可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到。

text: Yes, but first, Phil, I have a question for you about the Global Flourishing Study.
zh: 好的,不过Phil,我先问你一个关于“全球蓬勃发展研究”的问题。

text: In In terms of overall flourishing, which country do you think came out on top?
zh: 就整体蓬勃发展程度而言,你觉得哪个国家排名第一?

text: Was it a Indonesia, b Denmark, or c Brazil?
zh: 是a印度尼西亚,b丹麦,还是c巴西?

text: Ooh, I'm going to guess b Denmark, I think.
zh: 哦,我猜是b丹麦吧。

text: OK, Phil, well, we'll find out the correct answer later in the programme.
zh: 好的,Phil,我们稍后在节目中揭晓正确答案。

text: Now the study investigated six areas of life, including health, finances, social relationships and religion.
zh: 这项研究调查了生活的六个方面,包括健康、财务、社会关系和宗教信仰。

text: Let's listen as Claudia Hammond discusses some of the findings with psychologist Dr. Peter Olusaga for BBC Radio 4's All In The Mind.
zh: 我们来听听Claudia Hammond在BBC第四台《All in the Mind》节目中与心理学家Peter Olusaga博士讨论的一些研究发现。

text: What were the most interesting nuggets there? You've been diving into it.
zh: 你深入研究了这些数据,发现了哪些最有趣的“闪光点”?

text: Some of the sort of headlines were high -income countries, so Sweden, the USA, for example,
zh: 一些主要发现是:高收入国家,比如瑞典、美国,

text: scored higher on some of the more material aspects of flourishing, so things like finance. Middle -income countries,
zh: 在蓬勃发展中一些更物质化的方面得分更高,比如财务状况。而中等收入国家,

text: so Mexico, for example, scored more highly on things like meaning of relationships and kindness and social cohesion.
zh: 比如墨西哥,在人际关系的意义、善意和社会凝聚力方面得分更高。

text: Claudia asks about the study's most interesting nuggets.
zh: Claudia询问了这项研究中最有趣的“闪光点”。

text: A nugget means a small piece of something good, like wisdom, or precious, like a nugget of gold.
zh: nugget指一小块美好或珍贵的东西,比如智慧的“金块”或真正的金块。

text: While richer countries scored higher on material happiness, middle -income countries did better in terms of kindness and social connection.
zh: 富裕国家在物质层面的幸福感上得分更高,而中等收入国家在善意和社会联系方面表现更好。

text: There were interesting differences between men and women. For example, in Japan,
zh: 男女之间也存在有趣的差异。例如在日本,

text: women flourished more than men, while in Brazil the opposite was true.
zh: 女性比男性更蓬勃发展,而在巴西情况正好相反。

text: And across all countries, flourishing increased after the age of 50.
zh: 而在所有国家中,50岁以后人们的蓬勃发展程度都有所提升。

text: So, does flourishing depend on your nationality?
zh: 那么,蓬勃发展是否取决于国籍呢?

text: Or are there things anyone can do to feel happier wherever they live?
zh: 还是说,无论住在哪里,任何人都可以做一些事情来让自己更快乐?

text: Let's find out as Claudia Hammond talks more with Peter Olusaga for BBC Radio 4's All In The Mind.
zh: 我们继续听Claudia Hammond与Peter Olusaga在BBC第四台《All in the Mind》节目中的对话。

text: So, what can any of us do to help ourselves to flourish? That's the big question.
zh: 那么,我们每个人可以做些什么来帮助自己蓬勃发展呢?这是个大问题。

text: Well there's a few things that we can do and they are supported by research as well.
zh: 确实有一些方法是有研究支持的。

text: So things that are simple as just practicing gratitude, right,
zh: 比如简单地练习感恩,

text: being thankful for what we have as a way of increasing the amount of flourishing we have in our lives,
zh: 也就是对拥有的一切心怀感激,以此来增加生活中的蓬勃感;

text: showing kindness which helps us to develop connection with other people, and just celebrating our own accomplishments.
zh: 表达善意,这有助于我们与他人建立联系;还有庆祝自己的成就。

text: I think we've spoken on the programme before about savouring. Oh yeah we have, yeah savouring those good moments.
zh: 我记得我们节目里以前提到过“细细品味”。哦对,我们说过,细细品味那些美好的时刻。

text: Exactly, just taking the time to really appreciate some of those small wins.
zh: 没错,就是花时间真正去欣赏那些小小的胜利。

text: Claudia asks the big question. In other words, the thing everyone would like to know. How do people flourish?
zh: Claudia提出了这个“大问题”,也就是每个人都想知道的答案:人们究竟如何蓬勃发展?

text: There are simple things anyone can do, starting with gratitude, feeling thankful and appreciative for what you have.
zh: 任何人都可以做一些简单的事,首先是心怀感恩,对拥有的一切心存感激。

text: Being kind and connecting with others are also indicators of flourishing.
zh: 善待他人、与人建立联系,也是蓬勃发展的标志。

text: The study also recommends savouring life. To savour means to fully enjoy something by experiencing it slowly and mindfully.
zh: 这项研究还建议“细细品味生活”。savour意味着通过缓慢而专注地体验来充分享受某件事。

text: It involves taking time out to enjoy good moments, to stop and smell the roses, as we say.
zh: 它意味着花时间享受美好时刻,就像我们常说的“停下来闻闻玫瑰花香”。

text: This links to the phrase small wins, minor achievements or successes which bring you a little closer to your long -term goals in
zh: 这与“small wins”(小小的胜利)一词相关,指那些让你离长期目标更近一步的小成就。

text: life. There's an old saying, a journey of a thousand miles starts with a single step.
zh: 有句老话说:千里之行,始于足下。

text: And savouring each small win along the way is vital to flourishing.
zh: 而在这段旅程中细细品味每一个小小的胜利,对蓬勃发展至关重要。

text: seems to be a big new idea in some countries at least, which reminds me of your question, Georgie.
zh: 这似乎至少在一些国家是一个重要的新理念,这让我想起了你之前的问题,Georgie。

text: Yes, Phil, I asked you in the Global Flourishing Study
zh: 是的,Phil,我之前问你,在“全球蓬勃发展研究”中,

text: which country came out on top and the options were Indonesia, Denmark or Brazil.
zh: 哪个国家排名第一,选项是印度尼西亚、丹麦或巴西。

text: And I said Denmark. Which, Phil, I'm afraid that is the wrong answer.
zh: 而我说的是丹麦。Phil,恐怕这个答案是错的。

text: The correct answer is Indonesia.
zh: 正确答案是印度尼西亚。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with buzzword
zh: 好了,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,首先是buzzword,

text: – a word or expression on a certain topic that has become fashionable and much used.
zh: ——指某个话题上变得流行、被广泛使用的词语或说法。

text: A nugget is a small piece of something good or precious, such as a nugget of gold or a nugget of wisdom.
zh: nugget指一小块美好或珍贵的东西,比如一块“金块”或一句“智慧箴言”。

text: The big question is the thing everyone would like to know. Gratitude means being thankful for what you have.
zh: “大问题”指每个人都想知道答案的问题。gratitude指对所拥有的一切心怀感激。

text: If you savour something, you enjoy something as much as possible by experiencing it slowly.
zh: 如果你savour某样东西,就是通过慢慢体验来尽情享受它。

text: And finally, small wins are minor successes which contribute to a larger life goal.
zh: 最后,small wins指那些有助于实现更大人生目标的小小成功。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you want ways to help your English flourish,
zh: 我们的六分钟又到了,不过如果你想让自己的英语也“蓬勃发展”,

text: try the quiz and worksheet for this episode, available now on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 欢迎在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上做本集的测验、下载练习题。

text: Goodbye for now. Goodbye. Six Minute English, from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 再见啦。再见。这里是bbclearningenglish.com的《六分钟英语》。
